revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:府中八幡宮 (上越市)]]
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Fuchū Hachimangū | image = {{image requested|cat=上越市}} | coordinates = {{Coord|37|10|20.7|N|138|14|14.2|E|region:JP-15_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 3-5-8 Nishihonmachi, Jōetsu-shi, Niigata, Japan | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) | status = Echigo Province General Shrine | established = 720 (Yōrō 4) or 870 (Jōgan 12) | festivals = }}
=== Fuchū Hachimangū ===
'''Fuchū Hachimangū''' (府中八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|qid=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}, Japan. It is associated with the deified figure of Emperor Ōjin, known as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}.
=== History ===
Fuchū Hachimangū was established either in {{ill|720|en|720|qid=Q23539|lt=720|1=720}} (Yōrō 4) or {{ill|870|en|870|qid=Q23629|lt=870|1=870}} (Jōgan 12), following the enshrinement of spirits from either {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}} or {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qid=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}. It served as the general shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|qid=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}}, receiving the patronage of successive governors ({{nihongo|Kokushi|国司}}) throughout its history.<ref name="青年会">[https://www.naoetsu-crs.com/post_spot/hachiman/ Fuchū Hachimangū], Naoetsu Area Youth Association</ref>
During the medieval period, the shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Uesugi clan|en|Uesugi clan|qid=Q1150058|lt=Uesugi clan|1=Uesugi clan}}, a powerful feudal family. However, it was destroyed during the {{ill|Siege_of_Otate|en|Siege_of_Otate|qid=Q7510546|lt=Siege of Otate|1=Siege of Otate}}. In {{ill|1632|en|1632|qid=Q6865|lt=1632|1=1632}} (Kan'ei 9), it was rebuilt under the sponsorship of {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsunaga|ja|松平光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Mitsunaga|1=Matsudaira Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11104448}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takada Domain|en|Takada Domain|qid=Q839422|lt=Takada Domain|1=Takada Domain}}.<ref name="散歩">Niigata Historical Walk Editorial Committee, *Niigata Prefecture Historical Walk (Historical Walk 15)*, Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2009, pp. 27–28</ref>
=== Cultural Properties ===
* **Fuchū Hachimangū Wanikuchi** (Bell): Designated as a cultural property by Jōetsu City.<ref name="市1">[https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-pref012.html Fuchū Hachimangū Wanikuchi], Jōetsu City</ref> * **Fuchū Hachimangū Documents**: Designated as a cultural property by Jōetsu City.<ref name="市2">[https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-city015.html Fuchū Hachimangū Documents], Jōetsu City</ref>
=== Access ===
The shrine is a 9-minute walk from {{ill|Naoetsu Station|en|Naoetsu Station|qid=Q130065|lt=Naoetsu Station|1=Naoetsu Station}}.
== See Also ==
* Ichinomiya * Echigo Province Ichinomiya ** Yahiko Shrine ** Kota Shrine (Niigata) ** Amatsu Shrine
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Further Reading === * Niigata Historical Walk Editorial Committee, *Niigata Prefecture Historical Walk (Historical Walk 15)*, Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2009.
